State of California

Memorandum

To : Commissioners Gupta, Houston,
     McAndrews, Wetzger and Wade

Date : May 21, 1981

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
      Robert E. Leidigh

Subject: Review of Commission Regulations

Recent amendments to the Administrative Procedure Act require each agency to review its regulations during 1981. At the May meeting, the first group of regulations was presented to you for your review. At the June meeting, the second installment, concerning lobbyists, is presented to you.

As with the last month's review, the staff's procedures were to review our advice letter file and interview staff members to determine what regulations had been a source of confusion or difficulty in recent years. We also examined recent changes in the Act and court decisions to determine whether they necessitated any changes in our regulations. We tried to avoid the temptation to make merely stylistic changes; however, errors have been corrected.

Once these steps were completed, we translated our findings into proposed changes in the Commission's regulations. These changes generally may be broken down into two categories: Substantive -- changes involving the discretionary policy of the Commission to interpret the Act; and technical -- changes made necessary by recent amendments to the Act and court decisions, to correct minor inconsistencies among regulations, or to correct grammatical or punctuation errors.

In addition to the notice required by the Administrative Procedure Act, we noticed the proposed changes in the Lobbyist Bulletin, dated May 13, 1981.

Following is a summary of all the regulations reviewed, indicating whether the proposed changes to each regulation are essentially substantive or technical. The accompanying Statement of Reasons will explain the proposed changes in more detail.

Commissioners Gupta, Houston,
McAndrews, Metzger and Wade
May 21, 1981
Page Two


Substantive          Technical          No Change
Change               Change

18616               18239               18202
18623               18600               18615
                    18601               18618
                    18619               18620
                    18650               18622
                                        18630


To date, I have had several calls and one letter regarding the
proposed amendment to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18616, which would
require lobbyists to indicate, on their reports, the 5 or 10 bills
on which they have spent the most time during each reporting period.
Thus far, all the comments and questions have come exclusively from
"association" lobbyists, as opposed to "contract" lobbyists. An
"association" lobbyist is one employed full-time as a lobbyist by
one employer. "Contract lobbyists" represent numerous employers.
Specifically, calls have been received from the following:

    Jim Evans, United Transportation Union;
    Peter Schilla, Western Center on Law & Poverty;
    Don Reisner, California Railroad Association;
    Jim Eller, California Farm Bureau Federation;
    Jack Schreder, San Diego City School District;
    Dick Kelley, California Forest Protective Association;
    Emery Dowell, Blue Cross Plans of California (Letter);
    Bill Thomas, California Food Producers, Inc.

Only 3 lobbyists have made specific comments, another has written a
general letter. The comments all have expressed concern, reservation,
or displeasure with the new requirement. One lobbyist stated that
he currently keeps no time records, so the requirement would be
burdensome to him. He now reports his full salary, and does not
have to keep track of how much time he spends on each bill, since
he is full-time lobbyist for only one association. I asked him his
opinion of the possible alternative of using the standard of "most
important" as opposed to "most time." He felt this was too sub-
jective and also was concerned that his employers might be upset if
his determination of "most important" did not coincide with theirs.

Two other lobbyists suggested as an alternative to our proposal
that a de minimus test be devised to eliminate over-reporting of
minor bills on which little or nothing is done. For example elimi-
nate reporting when a lobbyist only contacts the author's office,

Commissioners, Gupta, Houston,
McAndrews, Metzger and Wade
May 21, 1981
Page Three


the author, or the committee consultant to find out what a bill
really does, and then takes no further action. Now lobbyists report
that, but if we instituted a de minimus test, they wouldn't have to
and would only report their major bills. The problem with this
suggestion is developing a workable de minimus standard. One phone
call from some lobbyists may be sufficient to kill a bill, for
others, much greater effort is involved. Furthermore, there now
exists a form of de minimus test since the Commission only requires
that lobbyists identify "specific legislation" s/he has
actively influenced or attempted to influence during the reporting
period." FEC Lobbyist Manual, page 14. Enclosed in your packet
is a copy of the complaint letter received to date, from Mr. Dowell,
which underscores the problem of over-reporting. When he reports
109 bills, the public won't know what bills he is actively or ex-
tensively working.

Another lobbyist raised the following concern with the "most
time spent" standard. He may spend 3 solid days sitting in Ways &
Means Committee waiting for 20 bills that he is following to be
heard. How does he attribute that time among these 20 bills? What
if he doesn't have to testify on 4 of the bills, but does on the
other 16?

REL:plh
Enclosures

SRU9S1J

ROOF OF PUBLICATION

(2015.5 CCP)

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

S.S.

County of Sacramento

has been adjudged a newspaper of general circulation by the

notice, of which the annexed is a printed copy (set in type not
smaller than non-pareil) has been published in each regular
and entire issue of said newspaper and not in any supplement
thereof on the following dates, to wit:

4/17

I certify (or declare) under penalty of perjury that the
foregoing is true and correct.

Signature

Date                Apr. 17, 1981

THE DAILY RECORDER

"Serving Sacramento Since 1911"

1115 "H" Street, P.O. Box 1048
Sacramento, CA 95805
(916) 444-2355

NOTICE OF PROPOSED CHANGES TO REGULATIONS
OF THE FAIR POLITICAL
PRACTICES COMMISSION

This sp [text continues but becomes less legible in right column with multiple sections about regulation amendments, authority, consistency, and reference information]

PUB. Tl [continues with notice text and hearing dates]

18:00 a.m., June 1, 2 and 3, 1981
State Personnel Board
801 Capitol Mall
Sacramento, California 95814

or

10:00 a.m., June 1, 2 and 3, 1981
State Building
107 S. Broadway
Los Angeles, California 90012

[Document continues with additional notice information and contact details]

DATED: April 18, 1981
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
SIGN: TOM K. HOUSTON Chairman

State of California

Memorandum

To     :  OFFICE OF ADMINISTRATIVE LAW                    Date  :  July 24, 1981


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Robert E. Leidigh

Subject:  Statement of Review Completion


     The Fair Political Practices Commission has completed its
review pursuant to Government Code Sections 11349, et seq. of
Chapters 2 and 6 of Title 2 of the California Administrative Code
the following regulations were included in this review:

     Regulation                              Disposition

     18202                                   No Change
     18239                                   Amended
     18600                                   No Change
     18601                                   Amended
     18615                                   No Change
     18616                                   No Change
     18618                                   No Change
     18619                                   Amended
     18620                                   No Change
     18622                                   No Change
     18623                                   Amended
     18630                                   No Change
     18650                                   Repealed

     Pursuant to Government Code Section 11347.3, the following
are attached to this memo:

     A.  Notices of Proposed Review of Regulations.

     B.  Original Statements of Reasons and proposals for
         changes in the regulations.

     C.  Written comments submitted on the regulations.  (The
         written comment consists of several letters; some
         public testimony was given on Regulation 2 Cal. Adm.
         Code Section 18616 only.)

Office of Administrative Law
July 21, 1981
Page Two


D.  Minutes of the public hearings held by the Commission
    on the regulations.

E.  Final Statements of Reasons and rhe regulations as
    finally acted upon by the Commission.

F.  Tapes of the Commission's public hearings on the
    regulations.

Note: This packet includes amended and repealed regulations
which were a part of the review process.  For your convenience,
the regulations and final Statements of Reasons are organized in
the same order and by the same categories as they are listed on
the Face Sheet, next attached.  Also, those regulations, for
which there was no change made in the review process, still
retain the authority and reference lines at the top.  Your editor
will change these to the new appropriate location.

RELplh
Enclosures

"Unapproved and Subject to Correction"

MINUTES OF MEETING

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

June 1, 1981
Sacramento, California


1. The meeting of the Fair Political Practices Commission convened
at 10:02 a.m. on June 1, 1981, in Room 150 of the State Personnel
Board, 801 Capitol Mall, Sacramento, California. The chairman
called the meeting to order. All members of the Commission
were present.

2. A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded by Mr. Metzger
to dispense with a reading of the minutes of the May 4, 1981,
meeting and approve the minutes as distributed. AYES: Gupta,
McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. The motion was carried.

3. The Commission considered the proposed amendment to 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18616 - to require lobbyists to designate
by use of an asterisk, those bills on which they have spent
the most time in each reporting period. Robert Leidigh made
the staff presentation. Public testimony was heard from:
Robert Moutain, California Manufacturers Association,
Allan E. Tebbetts, attorney for IGA; Suzanne Hutchinson,
California Chamber of Commerce; and, Don Collin, a registered
lobbyist. A motion was made by Mr. Wade and seconded by
Ms. McAndrews to reject this proposed amendment. AYES: Gupta,
McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. The motion was carried.
The staff was then instructed to research past lobbyist reports
to ascertain how many bills are reported by the "average"
lobbyist.

4. The Commission considered the following regulations:

a. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239 - amendment to correct a
typographical error which misplaced the term "or adminis-
trative." Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation.
No public testimony was presented. A motion was made by
Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Metzger to approve the
amendment. AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and
Houston. The motion was carried.

b. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18600 - amendment to correct a
typographical error and to correct a term so as to be
consistent with statutory definition. Robert Leidigh
made the staff presentation. No public testimony was

MINUTES OF MEETING
Page Two


                    presented.  A motion was made by Ms. McAndrews and
                    seconded by Mr. Houston that the amendment not be approved.
                    AYES:  Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston.  The
                    motion was carried.

     c.  2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18601 - to delete reference to
         Section 86202.  Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation.
         No public testimony was presented.  A motion was made by
         Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Metzger to approve the
         amendment.  AYES:  Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and
         Houston.  The motion was carried.

     d.  2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619 - to delete reference to
         Section 86202.  Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation.
         No public testimony was presented.  A motion was made by
         Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Wade to approve the amend-
         ment.  AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston.
         The motion was carried.

     e.  2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18623 - to delete "exchanges"
         from subdivision (b), make conforming changes to the
         constitution and to clarify to whom certain other provisions
         apply.  Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation.  No
         public testimony was offered.  A motion was made by
         Houston and seconded by Ms. Gupta to approve the amendment.
         AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston.  The
         motion was carried.

     f.  2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18650 - repeal the regulation.
         Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation.  No public
         testimony was presented.  A motion was made by Mr. Houston
         and seconded by Ms. McAndrews that the regulation be repealed.
         AYES:  Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston.  The
         motion was carried.

     g.  A motion was made by Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Metzger
         to approve the following regulations with no change:
         2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18202, 18615, 18618, 18620, 18622
         and 18630.  AYES:  Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and
         Houston.  The motion was carried.

5.   The Commission considered a memorandum from Robert Stern
     regarding possible statutory limitation on lobbyist contri-
     butions.  Barbara Milman made the staff presentation.  No
     public testimony was presented.  The Commission instructed the
     staff to ensure wider distribution of the proposal so that
     the Commission could receive more public input.  Further dis-
     cussion of the proposed legislative amendment was put off.

6.   The Commission discussed a memorandum from Barbara Milman
     regarding advising lobbyists that "gifts" made to elected
     officials in the form of picking up dinner tabs, etc. could
     not be treated or reported as contributions.  Barbara Milman
     made the staff presentation.  No public testimony was offered.

MINUTES OF MEETING
Page Three


     A motion was made by Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Wade
that a short article be published in the Lobbyist Bulletin
providing such advice.  AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger,
Wade and Houston.  The motion was carried.

7.  The Commission adjourned for lunch at 11:50 a.m. to reconvene
in Executive Session at 1:15 p.m., the public session to
reconvene at 1:45 p.m.

8.  The Commission considered 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18430.
Barbara Milman made the staff presentation.  Public testimony
was heard from John Hodgson, Dobbs & Nielsen.  A motion was
made by Mr. Houston and seconded by Ms. Gupta to set up a
task force of outside experts to assist the staff in formulating
a new draft regulation and accompanying draft reporting forms.
AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston.  The
motion was carried.

9.  The Commission considered the following regulations:

   a.  2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18306 - compensation and reim-
       bursement for members of the FPPC.  Barbara Milman made
       the staff presentation.  No public testimony was offered.
       A motion was made by Ms. McAndrews and seconded by Mr. Metzger
       to amend subsection (a)(2) redefining "necessary travel."
       AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger and Wade.  ABSTAIN: Houston.
       The motion was carried.  A motion was made by Mr. Houston
       and seconded by Ms. Gupta to amend subsection (b)(2)
       deleting "while traveling" for reimbursement of telephone
       expenses and to adopt the regulation as so amended.
       AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston.  The
       motion was carried.

   b.  2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18322 - procedures for hearings
       on opinion requests.  Barbara Milman made the staff presenta-
       tion.  No public testimony was offered.  A motion was made
       by Mr. Houston and seconded by Ms. McAndrews to remove the
       5 day time limits for submitting written materials to the
       Commission in subsections (b) and (d).  AYES: McAndrews
       and Houston.  NOES: Gupta, Metzger and Wade.  The motion
       was defeated.  A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded
       by Mr. Houston to delete the last sentence in subsection (d)
       and to add subsection (e) stating that all time limits
       imposed by the regulation might be waived by the Commission,
       and to adopt the regulation as amended.  AYES: Gupta,
       Metzger, Wade and Houston.  NOES: McAndrews.  The motion
       was carried.

MINUTES OF MEETING
Page Four


C.  A motion was made by Ms. McAndrews and seconded by
Ms. Gupta to approve all housekeeping regulations as pre-
sented by staff and to amend 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18351 to add the position of Special Assistant to the
Commission to the list of "designated positions" and
disclosure categories of the PPPC's Conflict of Interest
Code. AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston.
The motion was carried.

10. A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded by Ms. McAndrews to
approve the Conflict of Interest Codes and amendments to
Conflict of Interest Codes as presented by the staff.
AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. The
motion was carried. The following were approved:

Conflict of Interest Codes

    Office of Administrative Law
    Metropolitan Transportation Commission
    North Kern Vocational Training Center
    Central Coast Cities Self-Insurance Fund

Amendments to Conflict of Interest Codes

    Serra Cooperative Library System
    Department of Motor Vehicles

11. The Commission considered a memorandum from Jay Greenwood
regarding a proposed study of campaign contributions received
by legislative candidates. A motion was made by Mr. Houston
and seconded by Mr. Metzger to approve the proposed study.
AYES: Gupta, Metzger and Houston. NOES: McAndrews and Wade.
The motion was carried.

12. A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded by Mr. Houston
to adjourn the public session and meet in executive session.
AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. The
motion was carried.

13. The meeting was adjourned at 4:33 p.m.

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance •• Administration •• Executive/Legal •• Enforcement •• Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           323-5901          322-6441           322-6444


LOBBYIST BULLETIN                                                Vol. 5, No. 1
                                                                 May 13, 1981


                         FPPC TO REVIEW AND AMEND
                         LOBBYIST REGULATIONS

The FPPC, along with all other state agencies, is undergoing a
complete review of its regulations (Cal. Adm. Code, Title 2,
Division 6). Those dealing with lobbying will be considered by
the Commission at its June 1st meeting. All lobbyist regulations
are proposed to be continued without change except those which
follow. Most of these changes are non-substantive and merely
reflect changes in the law or corrections of typographical errors.
The proposed change most likely to be of interest to lobbyists
is found in Section 18616. Written and oral comments are welcomed.
For further information concerning these proposed changes, contact
Robert E. Leidigh, Counsel, FPPC Legal Division, (916) 322-5901.

Amend Section 18239 - To correct a typographical error which
misplaced the term "or administrative" in subdivision (e)(2)(B)1.
rather than in subdivision (e)(2)(A)1. where it belongs.
(Government Code Sections 82002, 82004, 82032, 82039)

Amend Section 18600 - To correct a term so as to be consistent
with statutory definition. (Government Code Sections 82021,
86100-86111, 86200-86205)

Amend Section 18601 - To delete reference to Section 86202,
which was invalidated by the California Supreme Court, FPPC v.
Superior Court, 25 Cal.3d 33 (1979). (Government Code Sections
86100-86103, 86202)

Amend Section 18616 - To require lobbyists to designate, by use
of an asterisk, those bills on which they have spent the most
time in each reporting period. (Government Code Sections 86105,
86107)


                                (over)

Appendix 2

FOR FILING STATEMENT OF REVIEW COMPLETION
WITH THE OFFICE OF ADMINISTRATIVE LAW

statement by:

                                                              (Date)

Pursuant to Government Code § 11346 Notice of Proposed Regulatory Action must be submitted to OAL for any regulations
adopted, amended or repealed resulting from the review (see procedures outlined in OAL letter of July 25, 1980).

I. Cite all regulations to be repealed.
    2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18650




II. Cite all regulations to be amended.
    2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18239
                                18601

III. Cite all regulations retained. Attach a Statement of Findings summarizing why the regulations are retained. Provide
    reasons and related information for retaining. State reasons for rejecting public comments recommending repeal of
    regulations. Provide specific references where none now exist.
    2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18202         18618
                                18600         18620
                                18615         18622
                                18616         18630

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

FOR FILING ADMINISTRATIVE REGULATIONS

AND UNDER THE DIRECTION OF STATE

sections.question_synthetic: This document is not an advice letter seeking legal analysis but rather internal FPPC memoranda and meeting minutes regarding regulatory review and amendment processes under the Administrative Procedure Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a compilation of internal FPPC memoranda, meeting minutes, and regulatory materials rather than an advice letter. No traditional Q/A sections present. Contains May 21, 1981 memo to Commissioners on regulation review, proof of publication, July 24, 1981 memo to Office of Administrative Law, and June 1, 1981 meeting minutes. Primary focus is regulatory amendment process, not legal advice to external parties.

embedding.summary: This document collection contains FPPC internal memoranda from May-July 1981 regarding the Commission's regulatory review process, including staff presentations on proposed amendments to lobbyist reporting regulations, public testimony, and Commission meeting minutes documenting votes on regulatory changes.
